verses.text: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति।भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया।।18.61।।
verses.transliteration:
īśhvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ hṛid-deśhe ‘rjuna tiṣhṭhati
bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā
verses.word_meanings: īśhvaraḥ—the Supreme Lord; sarva-bhūtānām—in all living being; hṛit-deśhe—in the hearts; arjuna—Arjun; tiṣhṭhati—dwells; bhrāmayan—causing to wander; sarva-bhūtāni—all living beings; yantra ārūḍhani—seated on a machine; māyayā—made of the material energy

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.61।।हे अर्जुन ! ईश्वर सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हृदयमें रहता है और अपनी मायासे शरीररूपी यन्त्रपर आरूढ़ हुए सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंको (उनके स्वभावके अनुसार) भ्रमण कराता रहता है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.61।। हे अर्जुन (मानों किसी) यन्त्र पर आरूढ़ समस्त भूतों को ईश्वर अपनी माया से घुमाता हुआ (भ्रामयन्) भूतमात्र के हृदय में स्थित रहता है।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: The Lord, O Arjuna, abides in the heart of every being, spinning them round and round, as if mounted on a wheel, by His power.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: O Arjuna, the Lord resides in the region of the hearts of all creatures, revolving through Maya all the creatures as though mounted on a machine!

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings, by His illusory power, to revolve as if mounted on a machine.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: O Arjuna! This Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, causing, by His trick of illusion, all beings to whirl round as if they are mounted on a revolving mechanical contrivance.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: God dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna! He causes them to revolve, as it were, on a wheel, by His mystic power.

verses.text: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम्।।18.62।।
verses.transliteration:
tam eva śharaṇaṁ gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata
tat-prasādāt parāṁ śhāntiṁ sthānaṁ prāpsyasi śhāśhvatam
verses.word_meanings: tam—unto him; eva—only; śharaṇam gachchha—surrender; sarva-bhāvena—whole-heartedly; bhārata—Arjun, the son of Bharat; tat-prasādāt—by his grace; parām—supreme; śhāntim—peace; sthānam—the abode; prāpsyasi—you will attain; śhāśhvatam—eternal

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.62।।हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन ! तू सर्वभावसे उस ईश्वरकी ही शरणमें चला जा। उसकी कृपासे तू परमशान्ति-(संसारसे सर्वथा उपरति-) को और अविनाशी परमपदको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.62।। हे भारत ! तुम सम्पूर्ण भाव से उसी (ईश्वर) की शरण में जाओ। उसके प्रसाद से तुम परम शान्ति और शाश्वत स्थान को प्राप्त करोगे।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Seek refuge in Him alone, O Arjuna, with the entirety of your being. By His grace, you shall find supreme peace and an eternal abode.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Take refuge in Him alone with your whole being, O scion of the Bharata dynasty. Through His grace, you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Fly to Him for refuge with all your being, O Arjuna; by His grace you will obtain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: To Him alone, O descendant of Bharata, you must go for refuge with all your thoughts. Through My grace, you will attain success and the eternal abode.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: With all your strength, fly to Him and surrender yourself, and by His grace you will attain Supreme Peace and reach the Eternal Home.

verses.text: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया।विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु।।18.63।।
verses.transliteration:
iti te jñānam ākhyātaṁ guhyād guhyataraṁ mayā
vimṛiśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru
verses.word_meanings: iti—thus; te—to you; jñānam—knowledge; ākhyātam—explained; guhyāt—than secret knowledge; guhya-taram—still more secret knowledge; mayā—by me; vimṛiśhya—pondering; etat—on this; aśheṣheṇa—completely; yathā—as; ichchhasi—you wish; tathā—so; kuru—do

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.63।।यह गुह्यसे भी गुह्यतर (शरणागतिरूप) ज्ञान मैंने तुझे कह दिया। अब तू इसपर अच्छी तरहसे विचार करके जैसा चाहता है, वैसा कर।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.63।। इस प्रकार समस्त गोपनीयों से अधिक गुह्य ज्ञान मैंने तुमसे कहा; इस पर पूर्ण विचार (विमृश्य) करने के पश्चात् तुम्हारी जैसी इच्छा हो, वैसा तुम करो।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Thus, the knowledge, the mystery of mysteries, has been declared to you by Me. Reflecting on it fully, do as you will.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: To you I have imparted this knowledge, which is more secret than any secret. Ponder over this as a whole and do as you like.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Thus, wisdom more secret than secrecy itself has been declared to you by me. Reflect on it fully, then act as you wish.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Thus, the path of wisdom, a better secret than all other secrets, has been expounded to you by Me; comprehend it fully and then act as you wish.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Thus, I have revealed to you the truth, the mystery of mysteries. Having thought it over, you are free to act as you wish.

verses.text: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम्।।18.64।।
verses.transliteration:
sarva-guhyatamaṁ bhūyaḥ śhṛiṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ
iṣhṭo ‘si me dṛiḍham iti tato vakṣhyāmi te hitam
verses.word_meanings: sarva-guhya-tamam—the most confidential of all; bhūyaḥ—again; śhṛiṇu—hear; me—by me; paramam—supreme; vachaḥ—instruction; iṣhṭaḥ asi—you are dear; me—to me; dṛiḍham—very; iti—thus; tataḥ—because; vakṣhyāmi—I am speaking; te—for your; hitam—benefit

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.64।।सबसे अत्यन्त गोपनीय वचन तू फिर मेरेसे सुन। तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है, इसलिये मैं तेरे हितकी बात कहूँगा।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.64।। पुन: एक बार तुम मुझसे समस्त गुह्यों में गुह्यतम परम वचन (उपदेश) को सुनो। तुम मुझे अतिशय प्रिय हो, इसलिए मैं तुम्हें तुम्हारे हित की बात कहूंगा।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Hear again My supreme word, the most secret of all; as I am exceedingly fond of you, I am telling you what is good for you.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Listen again to My highest utterance, which is the most profound of all. Since you are ever dear to Me, I shall speak what is beneficial to you.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Hear again My supreme word, most secret of all; for you are dearly beloved of Me, I will tell you what is good.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Yet again, you must listen to My ultimate, supreme message—the highest secret of all. You are My dear one and have a firm intellect, so I shall tell you what is good for you.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Listen to My last word once more, the deepest secret of all; you are My beloved, you are My friend, and I speak for your welfare.

verses.text: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे।।18.65।।
verses.transliteration:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru
mām evaiṣhyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo ‘si me
verses.word_meanings: mat-manāḥ—thinking of me; bhava—be; mat-bhaktaḥ—my devotee; mat-yājī—worship me; mām—to me; namaskuru—offer obeisance; mām—to me; eva—certainly; eṣhyasi—you will come; satyam—truly; te—to you; pratijāne—I promise; priyaḥ—dear; asi—you are; me—to me

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.65।।तू मेरा भक्त हो जा, मेरेमें मनवाला हो जा, मेरा पूजन करनेवाला हो जा और मेरेको नमस्कार कर। ऐसा करनेसे तू मेरेको ही प्राप्त हो जायगा -- यह मैं तेरे सामने सत्य प्रतिज्ञा करता हूँ; क्योंकि तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.65।। तुम मच्चित, मद्भक्त और मेरे पूजक (मद्याजी) बनो और मुझे नमस्कार करो; (इस प्रकार) तुम मुझे ही प्राप्त होगे; यह मैं तुम्हे सत्य वचन देता हूँ,(क्योंकि) तुम मेरे प्रिय हो।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Focus your mind on Me. Be My devotee. Worship Me. Prostrate before Me. You will come to Me alone. I promise you truly; for you are dear to Me.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, offer sacrifices to Me, and bow down to Me. Thus, you will come to Me alone. I promise you this truth, for you are dear to Me.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. You will come to Me; I truly promise you this, for you are dear to Me.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; offer oblations to Me and bow down to Me; you will come to Me alone. I promise you this truly, for you are dear to Me.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Dedicate yourself to Me, worship Me, sacrifice all for Me, prostrate yourself before Me, and you will surely come to Me. I truly pledge to you; you are My beloved.

verses.text: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः।।18.66।।
verses.transliteration:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ
verses.word_meanings: sarva-dharmān—all varieties of dharmas; parityajya—abandoning; mām—unto me; ekam—only; śharaṇam—take refuge; vraja—take; aham—I; tvām—you; sarva—all; pāpebhyaḥ—from sinful reactions; mokṣhayiṣhyāmi—shall liberate; mā—do not; śhuchaḥ—fear

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.66।।सम्पूर्ण धर्मोंका आश्रय छोड़कर तू केवल मेरी शरणमें आ जा। मैं तुझे सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त कर दूँगा, चिन्ता मत कर।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.66।। सब धर्मों का परित्याग करके तुम एक मेरी ही शरण में आओ, मैं तुम्हें समस्त पापों से मुक्त कर दूँगा, तुम शोक मत करो।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Relinquishing all Dharmas completely, seek Me alone for refuge. I will release you from all sins; grieve not.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Abandon all forms of rites and duties, and take refuge in Me alone. I will free you from all sins; do not grieve.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Abandon all duties and take refuge in Me alone; I will liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Abandon all attributes and come to Me as your only refuge; I will rescue you from all sins; do not be sorrowful.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Give up your earthly duties, surrender yourself to Me alone. Do not be anxious; I will absolve you from all your sins.

verses.text: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन।न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति।।18.67।।
verses.transliteration:
idaṁ te nātapaskyāya nābhaktāya kadāchana
na chāśhuśhruṣhave vāchyaṁ na cha māṁ yo ‘bhyasūtayi
verses.word_meanings: idam—this; te—by you; na—never; atapaskāya—to those who are not austere; na—never; abhaktāya—to those who are not devoted; kadāchana—at any time; na—never; cha—also; aśhuśhrūṣhave—to those who are averse to listening (to spiritual topics); vāchyam—to be spoken; na—never; cha—also; mām—toward me; yaḥ—who; abhyasūyati—those who are envious

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.67।।यह सर्वगुह्यतम वचन अतपस्वीको मत कहना; अभक्तको कभी मत कहना; जो सुनना नहीं चाहता, उसको मत कहना; और जो मेरेमें दोषदृष्टि करता है, उससे भी मत कहना।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.67।। यह ज्ञान ऐसे पुरुष से नहीं कहना चाहिए, जो अतपस्क (तपरहित) है, और न उसे जो अभक्त है; उसे भी नहीं जो अशुश्रुषु (सेवा में अतत्पर) है और उस पुरुष से भी नहीं कहना चाहिए, जो मुझ (ईश्वर) से असूया करता है, अर्थात् मुझ में दोष देखता है।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Never should this be told by you to one who is not austere, who is not a devotee, nor to one who has no desire to listen, nor certainly to him who speaks ill of Me.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: This (that I have taught) should never be taught to one who is devoid of austerities, one who is not a devotee, one who does not render service, or one who cavils at Me.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Never speak this to one who is devoid of austerities or devotion, who does not render service, who does not desire to listen, or who cavils at Me.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: This knowledge is for you, and it should never be imparted to one who does not observe austerities; to one who has no devotion; to one who has no desire to listen; and to one who is indignant towards Me.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Do not speak this to one who has not practiced austerities, or to one who does not love, or who will not listen, or who mocks.

verses.text: य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति।भक्ितं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः।।18.68।।
verses.transliteration:
ya idaṁ paramaṁ guhyaṁ mad-bhakteṣhv abhidhāsyati
bhaktiṁ mayi parāṁ kṛitvā mām evaiṣhyaty asanśhayaḥ
verses.word_meanings: yaḥ—who; idam—this; paramam—most; guhyam—confidential knowledge; mat-bhakteṣhu—amongst my devotees; abhidhāsyati—teaches; bhaktim—greatest act of love; mayi—to me; parām—transcendental; kṛitvā—doing; mām—to me; eva—certainly; eṣhyati—comes; asanśhayaḥ—without doubt

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.68।।मेरेमें पराभक्ति करके जो इस परम गोपनीय संवाद-(गीता-ग्रन्थ) को मेरे भक्तोंमें कहेगा, वह मुझे ही प्राप्त होगा -- इसमें कोई सन्देह नहीं है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.68।। जो पुरुष मुझसे परम प्रेम (परा भक्ति) करके इस परम गुह्य ज्ञान का उपदेश मेरे भक्तों को देता है, वह नि:सन्देह मुझे ही प्राप्त होता है।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: He who proclaims this supreme mystery among My devotees shall come to Me, acquiring supreme devotion towards Me. There is no doubt about this.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: He who, entertaining supreme devotion to Me, speaks of this highest secret to My devotees, will without doubt reach Me alone.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: He who, with supreme devotion to Me, teaches this supreme secret to My devotees, shall undoubtedly come to Me.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Whoever declares this highest secret to My devotees, cultivating an utmost devotion towards Me and not entertaining any doubt, shall reach Me.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: But he who teaches this great secret to My devotees, his is the highest devotion, and he shall surely come to Me.

verses.text: न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्िचन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः।भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि।।18.69।।
verses.transliteration:
na cha tasmān manuṣhyeṣhu kaśhchin me priya-kṛittamaḥ
bhavitā na cha me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi
verses.word_meanings: na—no; cha—and; tasmāt—than them; manuṣhyeṣhu—amongst human beings; kaśhchit—anyone; me—to me; priya-kṛit-tamaḥ—more dear; bhavitā—will be; na—never; cha—and; me—to me; tasmāt—than them; anyaḥ—another; priya-taraḥ—dearer; bhuvi—on this earth

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.69।।उसके समान मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय कार्य करनेवाला मनुष्योंमें कोई भी नहीं है और इस भूमण्डलपर उसके समान मेरा दूसरा कोई प्रियतर होगा भी नहीं।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.69।। न तो उससे बढ़कर मेरा अतिशय प्रिय कार्य करने वाला मनुष्यों में कोई है और न उससे बढ़कर मेरा प्रिय इस पृथ्वी पर दूसरा कोई होगा।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: There is no one among men who does dearer service to Me than him. Nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: And compared to him, no other human is better at accomplishing what is dear to Me. Furthermore, there will be no one else in the world dearer to Me than him.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: There is no one among men who does service dearer to Me, nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: And, except for him, there would be no one among men who is the best performer of what is dear to Me; and there shall be no one else dearer to Me on earth.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: There is no one among men who can perform a service dearer to Me than this, nor is there any man on earth more beloved by Me than him.

verses.text: अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः।ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः।।18.70।।
verses.transliteration:
adhyeṣhyate cha ya imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṁvādam āvayoḥ
jñāna-yajñena tenāham iṣhṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ
verses.word_meanings: adhyeṣhyate—study; cha—and; yaḥ—who; imam—this; dharmyam—sacred; saṁvādam—dialogue; āvayoḥ—of ours; jñāna—of knowledge; yajñena-tena—through the sacrifice of knowledge; aham—I; iṣhṭaḥ—worshipped; syām—shall be; iti—such; me—my; matiḥ—opinion

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.70।।जो मनुष्य हम दोनोंके इस धर्ममय संवादका अध्ययन करेगा, उसके द्वारा भी मैं ज्ञानयज्ञसे पूजित होऊँगा -- ऐसा मेरा मत है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.70।। जो पुरुष, हम दोनों के इस धर्ममय संवाद का पठन करेगा, उसके द्वारा मैं ज्ञानयज्ञ से पूजित होऊँगा - ऐसा मेरा मत है।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: And he who will study our dialogue, which is consistent with Dharma, by him I shall be worshipped through the sacrifice of knowledge; that is My view.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: And he who will study this sacred conversation between us two, which is conducive to virtue, by him I shall be adored through the Sacrifice of Knowledge. This is My judgment.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: And he who studies this sacred dialogue of ours, by him I shall have been worshipped through the sacrifice of wisdom; such is my conviction.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Whoever would learn this sacred dialogue of both of us, by them I am delighted through the knowledge-sacrifice: This is my opinion.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: He who will study this spiritual discourse of ours, I assure you, they shall thereby worship Me at the altar of Wisdom.

verses.text: श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श्रृणुयादपि यो नरः।सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम्।।18.71।।
verses.transliteration:
śhraddhāvān anasūyaśh cha śhṛiṇuyād api yo naraḥ
so ‘pi muktaḥ śhubhāl lokān prāpnuyāt puṇya-karmaṇām
verses.word_meanings: śhraddhā-vān—faithful; anasūyaḥ—without envy; cha—and; śhṛiṇuyāt—listen; api—certainly; yaḥ—who; naraḥ—a person; saḥ—that person; api—also; muktaḥ—liberated; śhubhān—the auspicious; lokān—abodes; prāpnuyāt—attain; puṇya-karmaṇām—of the pious

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.71।।श्रद्धावान् और दोषदृष्टिसे रहित जो मनुष्य इस गीता-ग्रन्थको सुन भी लेगा, वह भी सम्पूर्ण पापोंसे मुक्त होकर पुण्यकारियोंके शुभ लोकोंको प्राप्त हो जायगा।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.71।। तथा जो श्रद्धावान् और अनसुयु (दोषदृष्टि रहित) पुरुष इसका श्रवणमात्र भी करेगा, वह भी (पापों से) मुक्त होकर पुण्यकर्मियों के शुभ (श्रेष्ठ) लोकों को प्राप्त कर लेगा।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: And the man who listens to it with faith and without cavil, he too shall be released and shall reach the auspicious realms of those who have performed virtuous deeds.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Any man who, being reverent and free from caviling, listens to this (teaching) with reverence, he too, becoming free, shall attain the blessed worlds of those who perform virtuous deeds.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Also, the man who hears this, full of faith and free from malice, shall attain to the happy worlds of those of righteous deeds, and be liberated.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: A man who would at least hear this with faith and without indignation—he too, freed from sins, will attain the auspicious worlds of those who have performed meritorious acts.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Yes, he who listens to it with faith and without doubt, even he, freed from evil, will rise to the worlds which the virtuous attain through righteous deeds.

verses.text: कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा।कच्चिदज्ञानसंमोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय।।18.72।।
verses.transliteration:
kachchid etach chhrutaṁ pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa chetasā
kachchid ajñāna-sammohaḥ pranaṣhṭas te dhanañjaya
verses.word_meanings: kachchit—whether; etat—this; śhrutam—heard; pārtha—Arjun, the son of Pritha; tvayā—by you; eka-agreṇa chetasā—with a concentrated mind; kachchit—whether; ajñāna—ignorance; sammohaḥ—delusion; pranaṣhṭaḥ—destroyed; te—your; dhanañjaya—Arjun, conqueror of wealth

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.72।।हे पृथानन्दन ! क्या तुमने एकाग्र-चित्तसे इसको सुना ?और हे धनञ्जय ! क्या तुम्हारा अज्ञानसे उत्पन्न मोह नष्ट हुआ ?

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.72।। हे पार्थ ! क्या इसे (मेरे उपदेश को) तुमने एकाग्रचित्त होकर श्रवण किया ? और हे धनञ्जय ! क्या तुम्हारा अज्ञान जनित संमोह पूर्णतया नष्ट हुआ ?

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Have you heard this, O Arjuna, with a single-minded focus? Has your delusion, caused by ignorance, been dispelled?

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: O Partha, have you listened to this with a one-pointed mind? O Dhananjaya, has your delusion caused by ignorance been dispelled?

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Has this been heard, O Arjuna, with one-pointed focus? Has the delusion of your ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: O son of Prtha! Have you heard this with an attentive mind? O Dhananjaya! Has your strong delusion, born of ignorance, been completely destroyed?

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: O Arjuna, have you listened attentively to My words? Has your ignorance and delusion gone?

verses.text: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत।स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव।।18.73।।
verses.transliteration:
arjuna uvācha
naṣhṭo mohaḥ smṛitir labdhā tvat-prasādān mayāchyuta
sthito ‘smi gata-sandehaḥ kariṣhye vachanaṁ tava
verses.word_meanings: arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said; naṣhṭaḥ—dispelled; mohaḥ—illusion; smṛitiḥ—memory; labdhā—regained; tvat-prasādāt—by your grace; mayā—by me; achyuta—Shree Krishna, the infallible one; sthitaḥ—situated; asmi—I am; gata-sandehaḥ—free from doubts; kariṣhye—I shall act; vachanam—instructions; tava—your

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.73।।अर्जुन बोले -- हे अच्युत ! आपकी कृपासे मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया है और स्मृति प्राप्त हो गयी है। मैं सन्देहरहित होकर स्थित हूँ। अब मैं आपकी आज्ञाका पालन करूँगा।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.73।। अर्जुन ने कहा -- हे अच्युत ! आपके कृपाप्रसाद से मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया है, और मुझे स्मृति (ज्ञान) प्राप्त हो गयी है? अब मैं संशयरहित हो गया हूँ और मैं आपके वचन (आज्ञा) का पालन करूँगा।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, and, by Your grace, O Krsna, I have gained knowledge (Smrti). Freed from doubts, I stand steadfast, and I will fulfill Your will."

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Arjuna said, "O Acyuta, my delusion has been destroyed and my memory has been regained through Your grace. I stand with my doubt removed; I shall follow Your instructions."

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, for I have gained my knowledge (memory) through Your grace, O Krishna. I am now free from doubts. I will act according to Your word."

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Arjuna said, "My delusion is destroyed; I have regained my recollection through your grace, O Acyuta! I stand firm, free of doubts; I shall execute your command."

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Arjuna replied: "My Lord! O Immutable One! My delusion has fled. By Your Grace, O Changeless One, the light has dawned. My doubts have been dispelled, and I stand before You ready to do Your will."

verses.text: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः।संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम्।।18.74।।
verses.transliteration:
sañjaya uvācha
ity ahaṁ vāsudevasya pārthasya cha mahātmanaḥ
saṁvādam imam aśhrauṣham adbhutaṁ roma-harṣhaṇam
verses.word_meanings: sañjayaḥ uvācha—Sanjay said; iti—thus; aham—I; vāsudevasya—of Shree Krishna; pārthasya—Arjun; cha—and; mahā-ātmanaḥ—the noble hearted soul; saṁvādam—conversation; imam—this; aśhrauṣham—have heard; adbhutam—wonderful; roma-harṣhaṇam—which causes the hair to stand on end

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.74।।सञ्जय बोले -- इस प्रकार मैंने भगवान् वासुदेव और महात्मा पृथानन्दन अर्जुनका यह रोमाञ्चित करनेवाला अद्भुत संवाद सुना।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.74।। संजय ने कहा -- इस प्रकार मैंने भगवान् वासुदेव और महात्मा अर्जुन के इस अद्भुत और रोमान्चक संवाद का वर्णन किया।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Sanjaya said, Thus I have heard this wondrous dialogue between Vasudeva and the great-minded Arjuna, which makes my hair stand on end.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Sanjaya said, "I thus heard this conversation between Vasudeva and the great-souled Partha, which is unique and makes one's hair stand on end."

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Sanjaya said, Thus, I have heard this wonderful dialogue between Krishna and the high-souled Arjuna, which causes one's hair to stand on end.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Sanjaya said: Thus, I have heard this wonderful and thrilling dialogue between Vasudeva and the mighty-minded son of Prtha.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Sanjaya said: "I heard this rare, wonderful, and soul-stirring discourse between Lord Shri Krishna and the great-souled Arjuna.

verses.text: व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम्।योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम्।।18.75।।
verses.transliteration:
vyāsa-prasādāch chhrutavān etad guhyam ahaṁ param
yogaṁ yogeśhvarāt kṛiṣhṇāt sākṣhāt kathayataḥ svayam
verses.word_meanings: vyāsa-prasādāt—by the grace of Ved Vyas; śhrutavān—have heard; etat—this; guhyam—secret; aham—I; param—supreme; yogam—Yog; yoga-īśhvarāt—from the Lod of Yog; kṛiṣhṇāt—from Shree Krishna; sākṣhāt—directly; kathayataḥ—speaking; svayam—himself

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.75।।व्यासजीकी कृपासे मैंने स्वयं इस परम गोपनीय योग (गीता-ग्रन्थ) को कहते हुए साक्षात् योगेश्वर भगवान् श्रीकृष्णसे सुना है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.75।। व्यास जी की कृपा से मैंने इस परम् गुह्य योग को साक्षात् कहते हुए स्वयं योगोश्वर श्रीकृष्ण भगवान् से सुना।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: By the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this supreme mystery of Yoga, declared in person by Krsna, the Lord of Yoga.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Through the favor of Vyasa, I heard this secret concerning the supreme Yoga from Krishna, the Lord of Yogas, while He Himself was speaking!

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Through the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this supreme and most secret Yoga, directly from Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, Himself declaring it.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Through the grace of Vyasa, I have heard this highly secret supreme Yoga from Krsna, the Lord of Yogis, while He was imparting it personally.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Through the blessing of the sage Vyasa, I heard this secret and noble science from the lips of its Master, Lord Shri Krishna.

verses.text: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम्।केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः।।18.76।।
verses.transliteration:
rājan sansmṛitya sansmṛitya saṁvādam imam adbhutam
keśhavārjunayoḥ puṇyaṁ hṛiṣhyāmi cha muhur muhuḥ
verses.word_meanings: rājan—King; sansmṛitya saṁsmṛitya—repeatedly recalling; saṁvādam—dialogue; imam—this; adbhutam—astonishing; keśhava-arjunayoḥ—between Lord Shree Krishna and Arjun; puṇyam—pious; hṛiṣhyāmi—I rejoice; cha—and; muhuḥ muhuḥ—repeatedly

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.76।।हे राजन् ! भगवान् श्रीकृष्ण और अर्जुनके इस पवित्र और अद्भुत संवादको याद कर-करके मैं बार-बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.76।। हे राजन् ! भगवान् केशव और अर्जुन के इस अद्भुत और पुण्य (पवित्र) संवाद को स्मरण करके मैं बारम्बार हर्षित होता हूँ।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: O King, remembering again and again this wondrous and auspicious dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice time and again.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: And, O King, while repeatedly remembering this unique, sacred dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna, I rejoice every moment.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: O King, remembering this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I continually rejoice.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: O King! By repeatedly recollecting this wonderful, pious dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna, I am filled with delight again and again.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: O King! The more I think of that marvelous and holy discourse, the more I lose myself in joy.

verses.text:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः।

विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः।।18.77।।
verses.transliteration:
tach cha sansmṛitya saṁsmṛitya rūpam aty-adbhutaṁ hareḥ
vismayo ye mahān rājan hṛiṣhyāmi cha punaḥ punaḥ
verses.word_meanings: tat—that; cha—and; sansmṛitya saṁsmṛitya—remembering repeatedly; rūpam—cosmic form; ati—most; adbhutam—wonderful; hareḥ—of Lord Krishna; vismayaḥ—astonishment; me—my; mahān—great; rājan—King; hṛiṣhyāmi—I am thrilled with joy; cha—and; punaḥ punaḥ—over and over again

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.77।।हे राजन् ! भगवान् श्रीकृष्णके उस अत्यन्त अद्भुत विराट्रूपको याद कर-करके मेरेको बड़ा भारी आश्चर्य हो रहा है और मैं बार-बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.77।। हे राजन ! श्री हरि के अति अद्भुत रूप को भी पुन: पुन: स्मरण करके मुझे महान् विस्मय होता है और मैं बारम्बार हर्षित हो रहा हूँ।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: And, remembering over and over again that most marvelous form of Hari, great is my amazement, O King, and I rejoice again and again.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: O King, repeatedly recollecting that greatly extraordinary form of Hari, I am struck with wonder and rejoice again and again.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: And, remembering again and again that most wonderful form of Hari, I am filled with great wonder, O King; and I rejoice again and again.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: O great king! On recalling in my mind that extremely wonderful, supreme form of Hari, I am amazed and experience joy again and again.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: As I recall again and again the exceeding beauty of the Lord, I am filled with amazement and joy.

verses.text:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।

तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम।।18.78।
verses.transliteration:
yatra yogeśhvaraḥ kṛiṣhṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ
tatra śhrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama
verses.word_meanings: yatra—wherever; yoga-īśhvaraḥ—Shree Krishna, the Lord of Yog; kṛiṣhṇaḥ—Shree Krishna; yatra—wherever; pārthaḥ—Arjun, the son of Pritha; dhanuḥ-dharaḥ—the supreme archer; tatra—there; śhrīḥ—opulence; vijayaḥ—victory; bhūtiḥ—prosperity; dhruvā—unending; nītiḥ—righteousness; matiḥ mama—my opinion

translations.author: Swami Ramsukhdas
translations.text: ।।18.78।।जहाँ योगेश्वर भगवान् श्रीकृष्ण हैं और जहाँ गाण्डीवधनुषधारी अर्जुन हैं, वहाँ ही श्री, विजय, विभूति और अचल नीति है -- ऐसा मेरा मत है।

translations.author: Swami Tejomayananda
translations.text: ।।18.78।। जहाँ योगेश्वर श्रीकृष्ण हैं और जहाँ धनुर्धारी अर्जुन है वहीं पर श्री, विजय, विभूति और ध्रुव नीति है, ऐसा मेरा मत है।।

translations.author: Swami Adidevananda
translations.text: Wherever there is Sri Krsna, the Lord of Yoga, and Arjuna the archer, there is always fortune, victory, wealth, and sound morality. This is my firm conviction.

translations.author: Swami Gambirananda
translations.text: Where there is Krishna, the Lord of yogas, and where there is Partha, the wielder of the bow, there is fortune, victory, prosperity, and unfailing prudence. Such is my conviction.

translations.author: Swami Sivananda
translations.text: Wherever Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, is; and wherever Arjuna, the wielder of the bow, is; there is prosperity, victory, happiness, and a firm policy; this is my conviction.

translations.author: Dr. S. Sankaranarayan
translations.text: Where Krishna, the Lord of Yogis, remains, where the son of Prtha holds his bow, there lies fortune, victory, prosperity, and firm justice—so I believe.

translations.author: Shri Purohit Swami
translations.text: Wherever Lord Shri Krishna, the Prince of Wisdom, and Arjuna, the Great Archer, are, I am certain that good fortune, victory, happiness, and righteousness will follow.
